items.title: Macquarie Government launches Azure practice for agencies
items.summary: Australian agencies can now tap managed Azure services as Macquarie Government broadens its cloud offer amid pressure to modernise securely and cut costs.

items.title: RFID testing system lifts yield at Australian coal mines
items.summary: Australian coal producers using the RFID system have seen average yield gains of 6%, while lab turnaround has shrunk from weeks to minutes.

items.title: Cisco launches Antares AI models for code flaw pinpointing
items.summary: Security teams could cut hours from patching work as open-weight Antares models narrow flaws to the relevant code segment.

items.title: Ubuntu snap-confine flaw can give attackers root access
items.summary: A race condition in Ubuntu's snap-confine could let a local user seize root on default desktop installs, prompting urgent patching.

items.title: Denodo 9.5 adds metric views for AI data governance
items.summary: Metric views and a broader knowledge graph aim to keep AI tools, dashboards and business users aligned on trusted data definitions.

items.title: CrowdStrike warns of malware targeting AI coding tools
items.summary: Developers using AI assistants could face stolen credentials and poisoned repositories as the worm hides inside normal coding workflows.

items.title: 2026 holiday predictions: The evolving customer journey
items.summary: Rising fraud, shipping costs and AI-driven shopping are set to squeeze retailers' margins this holiday season unless customer data improves.

items.title: KnowBe4 launches AI video builder for ANZ training
items.summary: The new tool lets security teams in Australia and New Zealand update phishing training faster as AI-driven attacks become more personalised.

items.title: Lampion malware campaign targets users in Portugal
items.summary: Acronis said 94.6% of detections were in Portugal as the banking trojan used phishing lures and layered evasion to infect victims.

items.title: Professor Iain Murray named WA tech achiever of year
items.summary: A blind employment pathway he founded has helped lift job prospects for graduates, as Professor Iain Murray won Western Australia's top tech honour.

items.title: Ransomware Is Accelerating, But It's Not Because of AI
items.summary: Researchers pointed to fragmentation of the ransomware ecosystem, the emergence of new attackers, and expansion of attacks on less defended organizations.

items.title: Using LLMs to Find and Prioritize Vulnerabilities Is No Easy Task
items.summary: The latest large language models have high false-positive rates and fail to take into account the context of scans, leading to more work for AppSec professionals.

items.summary: Ivanti CSO Daniel Spicer says frontier models have shown surprising effectiveness in early stages, but cost and human-in-the-loop viability remain open questions.

items.summary: "The TFF Trap" uses fileless techniques and loaders with low detection rates to deploy various RATs and stealers, including Agent Tesla, Remcos, XWorm, and Best Private Logger.

items.summary: When chained together, the two vulnerabilities allow threat actors to gain root-level capabilities on SonicWall's mobile access appliances.

items.title: ICE to Pay Thomson Reuters $125 Million to Find ‘Voter Fraud’
items.summary: A new procurement record says ICE wants access to personal data, including names, Social Security numbers, and ethnicity, to investigate in part what the agency calls “voters fraud.” It also plans to use the data to investigate immigration fraud.

items.summary: Cutting-edge artificial intelligence models are deploying with more independence and less human oversight. Several state governments are trying to legislate transparency in their use.
